free_text: On the Absorption of Fast Neutrons by Heavy Nuclei
de L'Academie Des Sciences de L'URSS, vol.XLVIII,
.Estimated effective neutron energy is in the range
1 - 1.5 MeV. Maximal neutron energy is 2.7 MeV. This
energy is given in the Common Section
.To cut off thermal neutrons samples were covered
size 3 * 5 cm2, samples were covered by cadmium of
0.5 g/cm2 thickness and boron of 1.5 g/cm2 in form of
Br sample in form of PbBr(2).
Rare earth elements samples in form of oxides.
Cross section for n,gamma process was calculated as:
SIG(n,gamma) = alpha * N * A / s, where
s - the layer of the half-absorption of beta-electrons
in the material of the target ( found by experiment),
alpha - corrections coefficient, the same for all
.Corrections were made on finite exposition time,
on time delay between exposition and beginning of
beta counting, on the sample isotopic abundance,
on beta absorption in counter walls, on admixtures
In the Table of the article for these data the isotope
number is printed of low accuracy in first column.
Period in second column is given as 13.8 hours - such
HL is only for Zn-69-m ( 13.76 hr in Nucl.Wallet Cards
of 2011). So, this reaction is ZN-68(N,G)ZN-69-M.
DATA-MAX value is also printed with low accuracy -
M.M. Reaction was corrected ( see COMMENT):
